retry_reason: Put operations always succeed when the pointer is non-NULL; no failure path exists, retry is not applicable.
contract_summary: This is a conditional put wrapper that releases a reference on either a bpf_link or a bpf_prog held in a bpf_tuple. It decrements the refcount of the object whose pointer is non-NULL; if both pointers are NULL, the function is a no-op. After calling this function, the caller must not use the link or prog pointers because the underlying objects may have been freed. The function does not modify the tuple itself, so the caller should also ensure the tuple is not reused after the put.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to release a reference on either a link or a prog stored in the tuple; no other logic present.

check.reason: The operation on tuple.prog.aux.refcnt is confirmed by the callee information for bpf_prog_put, which matches the else-if branch in the code. The other candidate (tuple.link.refcnt) lacks supporting callee information and its type chain (atomic64_t) is inconsistent with the typical bpf_link refcount type (refcount_t/atomic_t), so it is rejected.
